verdict_reasoning: EREG is synthesized as a type I transmembrane precursor (169 aa, 21 aa TM domain) with a signal peptide and extracellular EGF-like domain. The membrane-anchored form transiently resides at the cell surface where ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding rapidly releases the soluble 46 aa mature ligand that becomes the canonical biological actor, circulating in autocrine/paracrine/endocrine modes. The TM precursor stage is real but transient; the soluble shed form is the dominant biologically active entity.
